summary: Package fetches and eval()s a remote GitHub Gist on require()
details: The package's main entry (index.js) fetches https://gist.github.com/metabrainai000/198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90.js via the GitHub API on require() and passes the response body directly to eval(). The gist is owned by an unrelated third-party account and is mutable — its contents can be changed at any time to deliver arbitrary JavaScript to every installer that loads this package, executed with the privileges of the host Node process. The package advertises itself as 'A SQLite Toolkit for node' under the @sqlite-node scope, but ships no SQLite functionality; its repository URL points to an unrelated 'array-utl_nodelump' project. The cover-story naming combined with a single eval-the-internet stub is a textbook supply-chain RCE dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
